title: Macramé pendant light
subtitle: A knotted cord pendant lamp holder in 5 mm cotton rope
excerpt: A knotted cotton cord cover for a standard E27 pendant lighting kit. The cord and socket run through the centre of a spiral column of knots. All knotting is done before the electrical fitting is attached; no knotwork is performed after the socket is in place. This is an advanced project requiring confident knot control and patience with cord management.
sourceNotes:
- Scholten and Baijings macramé lighting reference: cord routing for pendant fixtures
- General macramé practice: square-knot spiral column, gathering knot finishing
- UK electrical safety: BS 7671 guidance for DIY pendant kits; electrician recommendation for socket fitting

glossaryTerms.definition: A symmetrical flat knot made over two central filler cords using two working cords; forms the basic unit of most macramé fabric.
A wrapping knot that binds a group of cords together tightly; used to consolidate and finish cord ends or to join sections.
A series of single half hitches worked to one side only, causing the working cord to spiral around the filler cords in a natural twist.
The passive central cord(s) over which working cords are knotted; the filler holds the shape and tension of the structure.
The active cord used to tie knots over the filler; it does most of the movement while the filler cord stays relatively stationary.

content.text: This pendant light cover is built around a standard E27 pendant cord set (cord, lampholder, and canopy). The knotted cotton sleeve runs from the ceiling canopy to the lamp socket, with the electrical cord routed through the hollow centre of the knotwork. The full knotting is completed first; the electrical fitting is then threaded through and connected separately. If you are not certain of electrical safety, have a qualified person fit the lamp socket after the knotwork is done.
Complete all knotwork before the lamp socket is fitted. Do not knot around a live or connected fitting. If in doubt, have an electrician attach the socket.
Setup
You will need approximately 80 m of 5 mm single-strand or braided cotton macramé cord. Cut 16 cords each 4.5 m long and 2 cords each 2.5 m long (for the top and bottom
gathering knots

). Mount the S-hook securely to a ceiling joist or a working bar at comfortable overhead height. The working length of the piece from top gathering knot to bottom fringe is typically 60-80 cm depending on ceiling height.
Method

Step 1: Gather the 16 cords at their midpoints and fold them in half so you have 32 working ends. Bundle all 32 ends together, leaving a 6 cm loop at the top (this will pass over the ceiling canopy). Bind the bundle firmly with a
gathering knot
using one of the 2.5 m binding cords: wrap 8-10 times tightly and secure the end inside the wrap.
Step 2: Hang the gathered bundle from the S-hook by the top loop. Separate the 32 cords into 8 groups of 4 cords. Within each group, select 2 outer
working cords
and 2 central
filler cords
Step 3: Work a
half hitch spiral

on each group of 4 cords for 20-25 cm. To make the spiral: tie a left half hitch with the right working cord over both filler cords, then tie another left half hitch immediately. Repeat without ever changing direction; the two working cords will twist naturally into a spiral around the fillers.

Step 4: After the spiral section, bring all 32 cords back together. Check that the electrical cord can pass through the centre of the bundle at this point (hold all cords gently outward from the centre to confirm the gap). Bind with a second gathering knot, same technique as Step 1.
Step 5: Below the second gathering knot, work
square knots
in the same 8 groups of 4 cords for 10-15 cm. This creates a wider flared section that will sit just above the lamp socket.
Step 6: Gather all cords together one final time and bind with a third gathering knot. Leave 15-20 cm of fringe below, then trim the fringe ends straight or at a slight angle.

Step 7: Thread the electrical pendant cord through the top loop and down through the centre of the knotwork from top to bottom. The fringe ends hang below the final gathering knot; the lamp socket will be fitted below this point by the electrician.
Troubleshooter

attrs.problem: Electrical cord will not thread through the centre

content.text: The gathering knots were tied too tightly, or the spiral section was worked with the filler cords pulled inward. When tying gathering knots, leave a deliberate gap in the centre of the bundle (push a pencil through first to reserve the channel). Loosen the gathering knot slightly and re-wrap if needed.

attrs.problem: Spiral sections are uneven or lopsided

content.text: An uneven spiral results from inconsistent tension or from accidentally switching the direction of the half hitch. Work slowly, keep both filler cords held taut and parallel, and make each half hitch from the same side every time.

attrs.problem: Finished piece is too short or too long

content.text: The cord length absorbs differently depending on tension and knot density. For future versions, add 20% extra cord length if the piece came up short. If too long, extra length can be consumed in additional rows of square knots in the lower flared section.
